parsing_rationale: The document is a technical implementation plan for an Expense Tracker. I have mapped the existing sections to the required template fields, extracted the technical choices (Next.js, localStorage, etc.) as architecture_choices and tasks, and identified structural gaps since detailed API contracts, data schemas, and security identities are mentioned as 'future-ready' or 'minimal' but not explicitly detailed in this document.

project_info.brief_explanation: Single-user Expense Tracker using Next.js App Router with client-side persistence via localStorage.

sections.title: Summary
sections.raw_content: Small single-user Expense Tracker built with Next.js App Router. UI and persistence run in the browser using localStorage. Use server folder under `src/server` for shared types and validation utilities; route handlers and server actions are included as future-ready contracts but persistence remains client-side per requirements.

sections.title: Technical Context
sections.raw_content:
**Language/Version**: TypeScript (Node 18+/Next.js latest from package.json)

**Primary Dependencies**: Next.js (App Router), React, Tailwind CSS, Jest + React Testing Library for tests.

**Storage**: localStorage (client). Server folder `src/server` will host types and validation only.

**Testing**: Jest + React Testing Library (unit tests for validators and component behaviour).

**Target Platform**: Web (modern browsers).

**Project Type**: Web application (frontend-focused) with optional minimal backend route handlers for future server persistence.

**Performance Goals**: Small bundle, responsive UI, preserve Next.js server components where possible.

**Constraints**: No authentication. Follow project constitution (clean code, Next.js App Router, testing discipline).

sections.title: Constitution Check
sections.raw_content:
Gates from constitution:
- Next.js App Router & Server Components: using App Router — OK.
- Testing Discipline: plan includes unit tests; implementers must add tests before merge.
- Performance & Accessibility: keep UI light and accessible; use Tailwind for responsive styles.

No gate violations are expected. Tests are required in Phase 2 and must meet repository guidelines.

sections.title: Project Structure
sections.raw_content:
```text
src/
├── server/
│   ├── types.ts
│   └── validators.ts
app/
├── components/
│   ├── ExpenseForm.tsx
│   ├── ExpenseList.tsx
│   └── Summary.tsx
└── page.tsx
specs/001-expense-tracker/
├── plan.md        # this file
├── research.md    # Phase 0 output
├── data-model.md  # Phase 1 output
├── quickstart.md  # Phase 1 output
└── contracts/
    └── expenses-api.md
```

**Structure Decision**: Single-project structure with `src/server` for shared types/validators and `app/` for Next.js App Router code.

sections.title: Complexity Tracking
sections.raw_content:
No constitution violations requiring exception justification.

---

Phase 0 is next: research.md will resolve any minor technical unknowns and record decisions.

elements.content: Next.js App Router with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
Jest + React Testing Library for unit tests and component behavior
Use `src/server` for shared types and validation utilities to ensure future-readiness
Research phase to resolve technical unknowns and record decisions (research.md)
Implement unit tests before merge, following repository guidelines

structural_gaps.remediation_advice: Define the Expense entity structure (id, amount, category, date) in a dedicated data-model.md file.
Even if persistence is client-side, define the interface for the 'future-ready' route handlers in expenses-api.md.
Document the explicit absence of authentication as per requirements to avoid future confusion.
